title: Canon IV.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: To those who are altogether reprobate, and unrepentant, who possess the Ethiopian’s unchanging skin, and the leopard’s spots, it shall be said, as it was spoken to another fig-tree, “Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever; and it presently withered away.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: For in them is fulfilled what was spoken by the Preacher: “That which is crooked cannot be made straight; and that which is wanting cannot be numbered.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: For unless that which is crooked shall first be made straight, it is impossible for it to be adorned; and unless that which is wanting shall first be made up, it cannot be numbered.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Hence also, in the end, will happen unto them what is spoken by Esaias the prophet: “They shall look upon the carcases of the men that have transgressed against Me; for their worm shall not die, neither shall their fire be quenched; and they shall be an abhorring unto all flesh.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Since as by the same also has been predicted, “But the wicked are like the troubled sea, when it cannot rest, whose waters cast up mire and dirt.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: There is no peace, saith my God, to the wicked.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: What has been previously said of the lapsed, has been said of the repentant.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: But against those who are unrepentant, he brings forward the cursing of another fig-tree, to which the Lord said, because of its unprofitableness, “No fruit grow on thee hence-forward for ever.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: What has been previously said of the lapsed, has been said of the repentant.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Against those whom, from desperation or depraved opinion, are impenitent, and carry about with them perpetually the inherent and indelible blackness of sin, as of an Ethiopian’s skin, or the leopard’s spots, he brings forward the cursing of another fig-tree.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: To which the Lord said for its barrenness, “Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: And he says that in them must be fulfilled that word of the Preacher: “That which is crooked cannot be made straight; and that which is wanting cannot be numbered.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Then having explained these things, he subjoins the words of Isaiah.
